summary: Admin Impersonation feature fully tested and working. Backend: 9/9 API tests pass. Frontend: All UI features verified - Login as User button, impersonation banner, Return to Admin functionality all working correctly.

tests: POST /api/admin/impersonate/{user_id} - Impersonated token works for API calls (200)
POST /api/admin/impersonate/{user_id} - Block non-admin users from impersonating (403)
POST /api/admin/stop-impersonate - Logs end time and sets status to 'ended' (200)
GET /api/admin/impersonation-logs - Returns audit log entries with correct structure
'Login as User' button visible in dropdown for non-admin users in Settings > Users tab
'Login as User' NOT shown for admin-role users
Red impersonation banner appears at top after impersonating a user
Banner shows correct impersonated user name and role (kenway - vendor)
Banner shows admin name who initiated impersonation (System Admin)
'Return to Admin' button in banner is present and clickable
Return to Admin restores admin session and navigates back to settings
Impersonation redirects to correct page based on role (vendor -> vendor-portal)

critical_code_review_comments: Good: Security - Only admin can impersonate (403 for non-admins)
Good: Security - Cannot impersonate another admin (403)
Good: Audit logging - All impersonation activity logged with timestamps
Good: Session management - Admin token stored in sessionStorage, impersonated token in localStorage
Good: UX - Clear visual indicator (red banner) when impersonating
Good: Role-based redirection - Vendors go to /vendor-portal, sub_admins to /clients, others to /dashboard
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created - used existing users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin Impersonation feature complete. All backend endpoints working (/api/admin/impersonate, /api/admin/stop-impersonate, /api/admin/impersonation-logs). Frontend impersonation banner uses data-testid='impersonation-banner' and return button uses data-testid='stop-impersonation-btn'. Impersonation state stored in sessionStorage (admin_token, admin_name, impersonation_log_id).
